text: Mental toughness has a moderate to high correlation with athletic performance, with an overall effect size of r = 0.36.
The relationship between mental toughness and athletic performance is stronger in adults (r = 0.41) than in adolescents (r = 0.20).
Mental toughness has a stronger association with performance in individual sports (r = 0.73) compared to team sports (r = 0.21).
For endurance sports specifically, the correlation between mental toughness and performance is r = 0.32.

Mental toughness correlates more strongly with objectively measured performance (r = 0.33) than suggested by some measures, but subjective performance ratings show an even higher correlation (r = 0.62).
Combat sports show the highest correlation between mental toughness and performance (r = 0.73), followed by endurance sports (r = 0.32) and ball sports (r = 0.30).
specific_value: r = 0.73 combat, r = 0.32 endurance, r = 0.30 ball
